items.title: Empathy for and From Embodied Robots: An Interdisciplinary Review
items.author: C. Daryl Cameron, Alan R. Wagner, Martina Orlandi, Eliana Hadjiandreou, India G. Oates, Stephen Anderson
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Several years ago, the world was stunned when the cute robot HitchBOT was destroyed. Does empathy for robots—sharing experiences and feeling compassion—make sense for humans? How do people empathize with robots, and what are the ethical and practical ...</article>

items.title: Metacognition and Uncertainty Communication in Humans and Large Language Models
items.author: Mark Steyvers, Megan A. K. Peters
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Metacognition—the capacity to monitor and evaluate one’s own knowledge and performance—is foundational to human decision-making, learning, and communication. As large language models (LLMs) become increasingly embedded in both high-stakes and widespread ...</article>

items.title: A Framework for Automation in Psychotherapy
items.author: Zac E. Imel, Torrey Creed, Brent Kious, Tim Althoff, Dana Atzil-Slonim, Vivek Srikumar
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Psychotherapy is a conversational intervention that has relied on humans to manage its implementation. Improvements in conversational artificial intelligence (AI) have accompanied speculation on how technologies might automate components of psychotherapy, ...</article>
